\DoxyCodeLine{3747 \textcolor{comment}{/* ===============================================================}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3748 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3749 \textcolor{comment}{ *                          FONT}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3750 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3751 \textcolor{comment}{ * ===============================================================*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3752 \textcolor{comment}{/*  Font handling in this library was designed to be quite customizable and lets}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3753 \textcolor{comment}{    you decide what you want to use and what you want to provide. There are three}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3754 \textcolor{comment}{    different ways to use the font atlas. The first two will use your font}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3755 \textcolor{comment}{    handling scheme and only requires essential data to run nuklear. The next}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3756 \textcolor{comment}{    slightly more advanced features is font handling with vertex buffer output.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3757 \textcolor{comment}{    Finally the most complex API wise is using nuklear's font baking API.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3758 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3759 \textcolor{comment}{    1.) Using your own implementation without vertex buffer output}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3760 \textcolor{comment}{    -\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3761 \textcolor{comment}{    So first up the easiest way to do font handling is by just providing a}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3762 \textcolor{comment}{    `nk\_user\_font` struct which only requires the height in pixel of the used}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3763 \textcolor{comment}{    font and a callback to calculate the width of a string. This way of handling}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3764 \textcolor{comment}{    fonts is best fitted for using the normal draw shape command API where you}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3765 \textcolor{comment}{    do all the text drawing yourself and the library does not require any kind}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3766 \textcolor{comment}{    of deeper knowledge about which font handling mechanism you use.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3767 \textcolor{comment}{    IMPORTANT: the `nk\_user\_font` pointer provided to nuklear has to persist}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3768 \textcolor{comment}{    over the complete life time! I know this sucks but it is currently the only}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3769 \textcolor{comment}{    way to switch between fonts.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3770 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3771 \textcolor{comment}{        float your\_text\_width\_calculation(nk\_handle handle, float height, const char *text, int len)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3772 \textcolor{comment}{        \{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3773 \textcolor{comment}{            your\_font\_type *type = handle.ptr;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3774 \textcolor{comment}{            float text\_width = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3775 \textcolor{comment}{            return text\_width;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3776 \textcolor{comment}{        \}}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3777 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3778 \textcolor{comment}{        struct nk\_user\_font font;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3779 \textcolor{comment}{        font.userdata.ptr = \&your\_font\_class\_or\_struct;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3780 \textcolor{comment}{        font.height = your\_font\_height;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3781 \textcolor{comment}{        font.width = your\_text\_width\_calculation;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3782 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3783 \textcolor{comment}{        struct nk\_context ctx;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3784 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_init\_default(\&ctx, \&font);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3785 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3786 \textcolor{comment}{    2.) Using your own implementation with vertex buffer output}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3787 \textcolor{comment}{    -\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3788 \textcolor{comment}{    While the first approach works fine if you don't want to use the optional}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3789 \textcolor{comment}{    vertex buffer output it is not enough if you do. To get font handling working}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3790 \textcolor{comment}{    for these cases you have to provide two additional parameters inside the}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3791 \textcolor{comment}{    `nk\_user\_font`. First a texture atlas handle used to draw text as subimages}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3792 \textcolor{comment}{    of a bigger font atlas texture and a callback to query a character's glyph}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3793 \textcolor{comment}{    information (offset, size, ...). So it is still possible to provide your own}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3794 \textcolor{comment}{    font and use the vertex buffer output.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3795 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3796 \textcolor{comment}{        float your\_text\_width\_calculation(nk\_handle handle, float height, const char *text, int len)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3797 \textcolor{comment}{        \{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3798 \textcolor{comment}{            your\_font\_type *type = handle.ptr;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3799 \textcolor{comment}{            float text\_width = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3800 \textcolor{comment}{            return text\_width;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3801 \textcolor{comment}{        \}}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3802 \textcolor{comment}{        void query\_your\_font\_glyph(nk\_handle handle, float font\_height, struct nk\_user\_font\_glyph *glyph, nk\_rune codepoint, nk\_rune next\_codepoint)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3803 \textcolor{comment}{        \{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3804 \textcolor{comment}{            your\_font\_type *type = handle.ptr;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3805 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.width = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3806 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.height = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3807 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.xadvance = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3808 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.uv[0].x = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3809 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.uv[0].y = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3810 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.uv[1].x = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3811 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.uv[1].y = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3812 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.offset.x = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3813 \textcolor{comment}{            glyph.offset.y = ...;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3814 \textcolor{comment}{        \}}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3815 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3816 \textcolor{comment}{        struct nk\_user\_font font;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3817 \textcolor{comment}{        font.userdata.ptr = \&your\_font\_class\_or\_struct;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3818 \textcolor{comment}{        font.height = your\_font\_height;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3819 \textcolor{comment}{        font.width = your\_text\_width\_calculation;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3820 \textcolor{comment}{        font.query = query\_your\_font\_glyph;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3821 \textcolor{comment}{        font.texture.id = your\_font\_texture;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3822 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3823 \textcolor{comment}{        struct nk\_context ctx;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3824 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_init\_default(\&ctx, \&font);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3825 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3826 \textcolor{comment}{    3.) Nuklear font baker}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3827 \textcolor{comment}{    -\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3828 \textcolor{comment}{    The final approach if you do not have a font handling functionality or don't}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3829 \textcolor{comment}{    want to use it in this library is by using the optional font baker.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3830 \textcolor{comment}{    The font baker APIs can be used to create a font plus font atlas texture}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3831 \textcolor{comment}{    and can be used with or without the vertex buffer output.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3832 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3833 \textcolor{comment}{    It still uses the `nk\_user\_font` struct and the two different approaches}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3834 \textcolor{comment}{    previously stated still work. The font baker is not located inside}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3835 \textcolor{comment}{    `nk\_context` like all other systems since it can be understood as more of}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3836 \textcolor{comment}{    an extension to nuklear and does not really depend on any `nk\_context` state.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3837 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3838 \textcolor{comment}{    Font baker need to be initialized first by one of the nk\_font\_atlas\_init\_xxx}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3839 \textcolor{comment}{    functions. If you don't care about memory just call the default version}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3840 \textcolor{comment}{    `nk\_font\_atlas\_init\_default` which will allocate all memory from the standard library.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3841 \textcolor{comment}{    If you want to control memory allocation but you don't care if the allocated}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3842 \textcolor{comment}{    memory is temporary and therefore can be freed directly after the baking process}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3843 \textcolor{comment}{    is over or permanent you can call `nk\_font\_atlas\_init`.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3844 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3845 \textcolor{comment}{    After successfully initializing the font baker you can add Truetype(.ttf) fonts from}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3846 \textcolor{comment}{    different sources like memory or from file by calling one of the `nk\_font\_atlas\_add\_xxx`.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3847 \textcolor{comment}{    functions. Adding font will permanently store each font, font config and ttf memory block(!)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3848 \textcolor{comment}{    inside the font atlas and allows to reuse the font atlas. If you don't want to reuse}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3849 \textcolor{comment}{    the font baker by for example adding additional fonts you can call}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3850 \textcolor{comment}{    `nk\_font\_atlas\_cleanup` after the baking process is over (after calling nk\_font\_atlas\_end).}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3851 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3852 \textcolor{comment}{    As soon as you added all fonts you wanted you can now start the baking process}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3853 \textcolor{comment}{    for every selected glyph to image by calling `nk\_font\_atlas\_bake`.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3854 \textcolor{comment}{    The baking process returns image memory, width and height which can be used to}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3855 \textcolor{comment}{    either create your own image object or upload it to any graphics library.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3856 \textcolor{comment}{    No matter which case you finally have to call `nk\_font\_atlas\_end` which}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3857 \textcolor{comment}{    will free all temporary memory including the font atlas image so make sure}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3858 \textcolor{comment}{    you created our texture beforehand. `nk\_font\_atlas\_end` requires a handle}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3859 \textcolor{comment}{    to your font texture or object and optionally fills a `struct nk\_draw\_null\_texture`}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3860 \textcolor{comment}{    which can be used for the optional vertex output. If you don't want it just}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3861 \textcolor{comment}{    set the argument to `NULL`.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3862 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3863 \textcolor{comment}{    At this point you are done and if you don't want to reuse the font atlas you}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3864 \textcolor{comment}{    can call `nk\_font\_atlas\_cleanup` to free all truetype blobs and configuration}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3865 \textcolor{comment}{    memory. Finally if you don't use the font atlas and any of it's fonts anymore}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3866 \textcolor{comment}{    you need to call `nk\_font\_atlas\_clear` to free all memory still being used.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3867 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3868 \textcolor{comment}{        struct nk\_font\_atlas atlas;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3869 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_font\_atlas\_init\_default(\&atlas);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3870 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_font\_atlas\_begin(\&atlas);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3871 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_font *font = nk\_font\_atlas\_add\_from\_file(\&atlas, "{}Path/To/Your/TTF\_Font.ttf"{}, 13, 0);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3872 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_font *font2 = nk\_font\_atlas\_add\_from\_file(\&atlas, "{}Path/To/Your/TTF\_Font2.ttf"{}, 16, 0);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3873 \textcolor{comment}{        const void* img = nk\_font\_atlas\_bake(\&atlas, \&img\_width, \&img\_height, NK\_FONT\_ATLAS\_RGBA32);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3874 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_font\_atlas\_end(\&atlas, nk\_handle\_id(texture), 0);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3875 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3876 \textcolor{comment}{        struct nk\_context ctx;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3877 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_init\_default(\&ctx, \&font-\/>handle);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3878 \textcolor{comment}{        while (1) \{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3879 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3880 \textcolor{comment}{        \}}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3881 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_font\_atlas\_clear(\&atlas);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3882 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3883 \textcolor{comment}{    The font baker API is probably the most complex API inside this library and}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3884 \textcolor{comment}{    I would suggest reading some of my examples `example/` to get a grip on how}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3885 \textcolor{comment}{    to use the font atlas. There are a number of details I left out. For example}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3886 \textcolor{comment}{    how to merge fonts, configure a font with `nk\_font\_config` to use other languages,}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3887 \textcolor{comment}{    use another texture coordinate format and a lot more:}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3888 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3889 \textcolor{comment}{        struct nk\_font\_config cfg = nk\_font\_config(font\_pixel\_height);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3890 \textcolor{comment}{        cfg.merge\_mode = nk\_false or nk\_true;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3891 \textcolor{comment}{        cfg.range = nk\_font\_korean\_glyph\_ranges();}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3892 \textcolor{comment}{        cfg.coord\_type = NK\_COORD\_PIXEL;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3893 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_font *font = nk\_font\_atlas\_add\_from\_file(\&atlas, "{}Path/To/Your/TTF\_Font.ttf"{}, 13, \&cfg);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3894 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{3895 \textcolor{comment}{*/}}

\DoxyCodeLine{4060 \textcolor{comment}{/* ==============================================================}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4061 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4062 \textcolor{comment}{ *                          MEMORY BUFFER}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4063 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4064 \textcolor{comment}{ * ===============================================================*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4065 \textcolor{comment}{/*  A basic (double)-\/buffer with linear allocation and resetting as only}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4066 \textcolor{comment}{    freeing policy. The buffer's main purpose is to control all memory management}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4067 \textcolor{comment}{    inside the GUI toolkit and still leave memory control as much as possible in}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4068 \textcolor{comment}{    the hand of the user while also making sure the library is easy to use if}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4069 \textcolor{comment}{    not as much control is needed.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4070 \textcolor{comment}{    In general all memory inside this library can be provided from the user in}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4071 \textcolor{comment}{    three different ways.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4072 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4073 \textcolor{comment}{    The first way and the one providing most control is by just passing a fixed}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4074 \textcolor{comment}{    size memory block. In this case all control lies in the hand of the user}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4075 \textcolor{comment}{    since he can exactly control where the memory comes from and how much memory}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4076 \textcolor{comment}{    the library should consume. Of course using the fixed size API removes the}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4077 \textcolor{comment}{    ability to automatically resize a buffer if not enough memory is provided so}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4078 \textcolor{comment}{    you have to take over the resizing. While being a fixed sized buffer sounds}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4079 \textcolor{comment}{    quite limiting, it is very effective in this library since the actual memory}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4080 \textcolor{comment}{    consumption is quite stable and has a fixed upper bound for a lot of cases.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4081 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4082 \textcolor{comment}{    If you don't want to think about how much memory the library should allocate}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4083 \textcolor{comment}{    at all time or have a very dynamic UI with unpredictable memory consumption}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4084 \textcolor{comment}{    habits but still want control over memory allocation you can use the dynamic}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4085 \textcolor{comment}{    allocator based API. The allocator consists of two callbacks for allocating}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4086 \textcolor{comment}{    and freeing memory and optional userdata so you can plugin your own allocator.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4087 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4088 \textcolor{comment}{    The final and easiest way can be used by defining}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4089 \textcolor{comment}{    NK\_INCLUDE\_DEFAULT\_ALLOCATOR which uses the standard library memory}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4090 \textcolor{comment}{    allocation functions malloc and free and takes over complete control over}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4091 \textcolor{comment}{    memory in this library.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4092 \textcolor{comment}{*/}}

\DoxyCodeLine{4094     \textcolor{keywordtype}{void} *memory;}
\DoxyCodeLine{4095     \textcolor{keywordtype}{unsigned} \textcolor{keywordtype}{int} type;}
\DoxyCodeLine{4096     nk\_size size;}
\DoxyCodeLine{4097     nk\_size allocated;}
\DoxyCodeLine{4098     nk\_size needed;}
\DoxyCodeLine{4099     nk\_size calls;}
\DoxyCodeLine{4100 \};}

\DoxyCodeLine{4211 \textcolor{comment}{/*===============================================================}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4212 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4213 \textcolor{comment}{ *                      TEXT EDITOR}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4214 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4215 \textcolor{comment}{ * ===============================================================*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4216 \textcolor{comment}{/* Editing text in this library is handled by either `nk\_edit\_string` or}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4217 \textcolor{comment}{ * `nk\_edit\_buffer`. But like almost everything in this library there are multiple}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4218 \textcolor{comment}{ * ways of doing it and a balance between control and ease of use with memory}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4219 \textcolor{comment}{ * as well as functionality controlled by flags.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4220 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4221 \textcolor{comment}{ * This library generally allows three different levels of memory control:}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4222 \textcolor{comment}{ * First of is the most basic way of just providing a simple char array with}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4223 \textcolor{comment}{ * string length. This method is probably the easiest way of handling simple}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4224 \textcolor{comment}{ * user text input. Main upside is complete control over memory while the biggest}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4225 \textcolor{comment}{ * downside in comparison with the other two approaches is missing undo/redo.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4226 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4227 \textcolor{comment}{ * For UIs that require undo/redo the second way was created. It is based on}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4228 \textcolor{comment}{ * a fixed size nk\_text\_edit struct, which has an internal undo/redo stack.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4229 \textcolor{comment}{ * This is mainly useful if you want something more like a text editor but don't want}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4230 \textcolor{comment}{ * to have a dynamically growing buffer.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4231 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4232 \textcolor{comment}{ * The final way is using a dynamically growing nk\_text\_edit struct, which}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4233 \textcolor{comment}{ * has both a default version if you don't care where memory comes from and an}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4234 \textcolor{comment}{ * allocator version if you do. While the text editor is quite powerful for its}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4235 \textcolor{comment}{ * complexity I would not recommend editing gigabytes of data with it.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4236 \textcolor{comment}{ * It is rather designed for uses cases which make sense for a GUI library not for}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4237 \textcolor{comment}{ * an full blown text editor.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4238 \textcolor{comment}{ */}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4239 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#ifndef NK\_TEXTEDIT\_UNDOSTATECOUNT}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4240 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#define NK\_TEXTEDIT\_UNDOSTATECOUNT     99}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4241 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#endif}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4242 }
\DoxyCodeLine{4243 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#ifndef NK\_TEXTEDIT\_UNDOCHARCOUNT}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4244 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#define NK\_TEXTEDIT\_UNDOCHARCOUNT      999}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4245 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#endif}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4246 }

\DoxyCodeLine{4326 \textcolor{comment}{/* ===============================================================}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4327 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4328 \textcolor{comment}{ *                          DRAWING}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4329 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4330 \textcolor{comment}{ * ===============================================================*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4331 \textcolor{comment}{/*  This library was designed to be render backend agnostic so it does}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4332 \textcolor{comment}{    not draw anything to screen. Instead all drawn shapes, widgets}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4333 \textcolor{comment}{    are made of, are buffered into memory and make up a command queue.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4334 \textcolor{comment}{    Each frame therefore fills the command buffer with draw commands}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4335 \textcolor{comment}{    that then need to be executed by the user and his own render backend.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4336 \textcolor{comment}{    After that the command buffer needs to be cleared and a new frame can be}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4337 \textcolor{comment}{    started. It is probably important to note that the command buffer is the main}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4338 \textcolor{comment}{    drawing API and the optional vertex buffer API only takes this format and}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4339 \textcolor{comment}{    converts it into a hardware accessible format.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4340 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4341 \textcolor{comment}{    To use the command queue to draw your own widgets you can access the}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4342 \textcolor{comment}{    command buffer of each window by calling `nk\_window\_get\_canvas` after}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4343 \textcolor{comment}{    previously having called `nk\_begin`:}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4344 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4345 \textcolor{comment}{        void draw\_red\_rectangle\_widget(struct nk\_context *ctx)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4346 \textcolor{comment}{        \{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4347 \textcolor{comment}{            struct nk\_command\_buffer *canvas;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4348 \textcolor{comment}{            struct nk\_input *input = \&ctx-\/>input;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4349 \textcolor{comment}{            canvas = nk\_window\_get\_canvas(ctx);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4350 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4351 \textcolor{comment}{            struct nk\_rect space;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4352 \textcolor{comment}{            enum nk\_widget\_layout\_states state;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4353 \textcolor{comment}{            state = nk\_widget(\&space, ctx);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4354 \textcolor{comment}{            if (!state) return;}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4355 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4356 \textcolor{comment}{            if (state != NK\_WIDGET\_ROM)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4357 \textcolor{comment}{                update\_your\_widget\_by\_user\_input(...);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4358 \textcolor{comment}{            nk\_fill\_rect(canvas, space, 0, nk\_rgb(255,0,0));}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4359 \textcolor{comment}{        \}}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4360 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4361 \textcolor{comment}{        if (nk\_begin(...)) \{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4362 \textcolor{comment}{            nk\_layout\_row\_dynamic(ctx, 25, 1);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4363 \textcolor{comment}{            draw\_red\_rectangle\_widget(ctx);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4364 \textcolor{comment}{        \}}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4365 \textcolor{comment}{        nk\_end(..)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4366 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4367 \textcolor{comment}{    Important to know if you want to create your own widgets is the `nk\_widget`}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4368 \textcolor{comment}{    call. It allocates space on the panel reserved for this widget to be used,}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4369 \textcolor{comment}{    but also returns the state of the widget space. If your widget is not seen and does}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4370 \textcolor{comment}{    not have to be updated it is '0' and you can just return. If it only has}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4371 \textcolor{comment}{    to be drawn the state will be `NK\_WIDGET\_ROM` otherwise you can do both}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4372 \textcolor{comment}{    update and draw your widget. The reason for separating is to only draw and}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4373 \textcolor{comment}{    update what is actually necessary which is crucial for performance.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4374 \textcolor{comment}{*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4375 \textcolor{keyword}{enum} nk\_command\_type \{}
\DoxyCodeLine{4376     NK\_COMMAND\_NOP,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4377     NK\_COMMAND\_SCISSOR,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4378     NK\_COMMAND\_LINE,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4379     NK\_COMMAND\_CURVE,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4380     NK\_COMMAND\_RECT,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4381     NK\_COMMAND\_RECT\_FILLED,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4382     NK\_COMMAND\_RECT\_MULTI\_COLOR,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4383     NK\_COMMAND\_CIRCLE,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4384     NK\_COMMAND\_CIRCLE\_FILLED,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4385     NK\_COMMAND\_ARC,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4386     NK\_COMMAND\_ARC\_FILLED,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4387     NK\_COMMAND\_TRIANGLE,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4388     NK\_COMMAND\_TRIANGLE\_FILLED,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4389     NK\_COMMAND\_POLYGON,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4390     NK\_COMMAND\_POLYGON\_FILLED,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4391     NK\_COMMAND\_POLYLINE,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4392     NK\_COMMAND\_TEXT,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4393     NK\_COMMAND\_IMAGE,}
\DoxyCodeLine{4394     NK\_COMMAND\_CUSTOM}
\DoxyCodeLine{4395 \};}
\DoxyCodeLine{4396 }

\DoxyCodeLine{4647 \textcolor{comment}{/* ===============================================================}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4648 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4649 \textcolor{comment}{ *                          DRAW LIST}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4650 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4651 \textcolor{comment}{ * ===============================================================*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4652 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#ifdef NK\_INCLUDE\_VERTEX\_BUFFER\_OUTPUT}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4653 \textcolor{comment}{/*  The optional vertex buffer draw list provides a 2D drawing context}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4654 \textcolor{comment}{    with antialiasing functionality which takes basic filled or outlined shapes}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4655 \textcolor{comment}{    or a path and outputs vertexes, elements and draw commands.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4656 \textcolor{comment}{    The actual draw list API is not required to be used directly while using this}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4657 \textcolor{comment}{    library since converting the default library draw command output is done by}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4658 \textcolor{comment}{    just calling `nk\_convert` but I decided to still make this library accessible}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4659 \textcolor{comment}{    since it can be useful.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4660 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4661 \textcolor{comment}{    The draw list is based on a path buffering and polygon and polyline}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4662 \textcolor{comment}{    rendering API which allows a lot of ways to draw 2D content to screen.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4663 \textcolor{comment}{    In fact it is probably more powerful than needed but allows even more crazy}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4664 \textcolor{comment}{    things than this library provides by default.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4665 \textcolor{comment}{*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{4666 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#ifdef NK\_UINT\_DRAW\_INDEX}}

\DoxyCodeLine{5437 \textcolor{comment}{ *                          STACK}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5438 \textcolor{comment}{ * =============================================================*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5439 \textcolor{comment}{/* The style modifier stack can be used to temporarily change a}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5440 \textcolor{comment}{ * property inside `nk\_style`. For example if you want a special}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5441 \textcolor{comment}{ * red button you can temporarily push the old button color onto a stack}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5442 \textcolor{comment}{ * draw the button with a red color and then you just pop the old color}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5443 \textcolor{comment}{ * back from the stack:}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5444 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5445 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_style\_push\_style\_item(ctx, \&ctx-\/>style.button.normal, nk\_style\_item\_color(nk\_rgb(255,0,0)));}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5446 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_style\_push\_style\_item(ctx, \&ctx-\/>style.button.hover, nk\_style\_item\_color(nk\_rgb(255,0,0)));}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5447 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_style\_push\_style\_item(ctx, \&ctx-\/>style.button.active, nk\_style\_item\_color(nk\_rgb(255,0,0)));}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5448 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_style\_push\_vec2(ctx, \&cx-\/>style.button.padding, nk\_vec2(2,2));}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5449 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5450 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_button(...);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5451 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5452 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_style\_pop\_style\_item(ctx);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5453 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_style\_pop\_style\_item(ctx);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5454 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_style\_pop\_style\_item(ctx);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5455 \textcolor{comment}{ *      nk\_style\_pop\_vec2(ctx);}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5456 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5457 \textcolor{comment}{ * Nuklear has a stack for style\_items, float properties, vector properties,}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5458 \textcolor{comment}{ * flags, colors, fonts and for button\_behavior. Each has it's own fixed size stack}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5459 \textcolor{comment}{ * which can be changed at compile time.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5460 \textcolor{comment}{ */}}
\DoxyCodeLine{5461 \textcolor{preprocessor}{\#ifndef NK\_BUTTON\_BEHAVIOR\_STACK\_SIZE}}

\DoxyCodeLine{6022 }
\DoxyCodeLine{6023 \textcolor{comment}{/* ===============================================================}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6024 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6025 \textcolor{comment}{ *                              MATH}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6026 \textcolor{comment}{ *}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6027 \textcolor{comment}{ * ===============================================================*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6028 \textcolor{comment}{/*  Since nuklear is supposed to work on all systems providing floating point}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6029 \textcolor{comment}{    math without any dependencies I also had to implement my own math functions}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6030 \textcolor{comment}{    for sqrt, sin and cos. Since the actual highly accurate implementations for}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6031 \textcolor{comment}{    the standard library functions are quite complex and I do not need high}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6032 \textcolor{comment}{    precision for my use cases I use approximations.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6033 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6034 \textcolor{comment}{    Sqrt}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6035 \textcolor{comment}{    -\/-\/-\/-\/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6036 \textcolor{comment}{    For square root nuklear uses the famous fast inverse square root:}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6037 \textcolor{comment}{    https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fast\_inverse\_square\_root with}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6038 \textcolor{comment}{    slightly tweaked magic constant. While on today's hardware it is}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6039 \textcolor{comment}{    probably not faster it is still fast and accurate enough for}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6040 \textcolor{comment}{    nuklear's use cases. IMPORTANT: this requires float format IEEE 754}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6041 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6042 \textcolor{comment}{    Sine/Cosine}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6043 \textcolor{comment}{    -\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/-\/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6044 \textcolor{comment}{    All constants inside both function are generated Remez's minimax}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6045 \textcolor{comment}{    approximations for value range 0...2*PI. The reason why I decided to}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6046 \textcolor{comment}{    approximate exactly that range is that nuklear only needs sine and}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6047 \textcolor{comment}{    cosine to generate circles which only requires that exact range.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6048 \textcolor{comment}{    In addition I used Remez instead of Taylor for additional precision:}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6049 \textcolor{comment}{    www.lolengine.net/blog/2011/12/21/better-\/function-\/approximations.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6050 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6051 \textcolor{comment}{    The tool I used to generate constants for both sine and cosine}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6052 \textcolor{comment}{    (it can actually approximate a lot more functions) can be}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6053 \textcolor{comment}{    found here: www.lolengine.net/wiki/oss/lolremez}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6054 \textcolor{comment}{*/}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6055 NK\_LIB \textcolor{keywordtype}{float}}
\DoxyCodeLine{6056 nk\_inv\_sqrt(\textcolor{keywordtype}{float} n)}
\DoxyCodeLine{6057 \{}
\DoxyCodeLine{6058     \textcolor{keywordtype}{float} x2;}
\DoxyCodeLine{6059     \textcolor{keyword}{const} \textcolor{keywordtype}{float} threehalfs = 1.5f;}
\DoxyCodeLine{6060     \textcolor{keyword}{union }\{nk\_uint i; \textcolor{keywordtype}{float} f;\} conv = \{0\};}
\DoxyCodeLine{6061     conv.f = n;}
\DoxyCodeLine{6062     x2 = n * 0.5f;}
\DoxyCodeLine{6063     conv.i = 0x5f375A84 -\/ (conv.i >> 1);}
\DoxyCodeLine{6064     conv.f = conv.f * (threehalfs -\/ (x2 * conv.f * conv.f));}
\DoxyCodeLine{6065     \textcolor{keywordflow}{return} conv.f;}
\DoxyCodeLine{6066 \}}

\DoxyCodeLine{6068 nk\_sqrt(\textcolor{keywordtype}{float} x)}
\DoxyCodeLine{6069 \{}

\DoxyCodeLine{6071 \}}

\DoxyCodeLine{10039     \textcolor{comment}{/*  This algorithm for arc drawing relies on these two trigonometric identities[1]:}}

\DoxyCodeLine{10043 \textcolor{comment}{        Two coordinates (x, y) of a point on a circle centered on}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10044 \textcolor{comment}{        the origin can be written in polar form as:}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10045 \textcolor{comment}{            x = r * cos(a)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10046 \textcolor{comment}{            y = r * sin(a)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10047 \textcolor{comment}{        where r is the radius of the circle,}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10048 \textcolor{comment}{            a is the angle between (x, y) and the origin.}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10049 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10050 \textcolor{comment}{        This allows us to rotate the coordinates around the}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10051 \textcolor{comment}{        origin by an angle b using the following transformation:}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10052 \textcolor{comment}{            x' = r * cos(a + b) = x * cos(b) -\/ y * sin(b)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10053 \textcolor{comment}{            y' = r * sin(a + b) = y * cos(b) + x * sin(b)}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10054 \textcolor{comment}{}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10055 \textcolor{comment}{        [1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List\_of\_trigonometric\_identities\#Angle\_sum\_and\_difference\_identities}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10056 \textcolor{comment}{    */}}
\DoxyCodeLine{10057     \{\textcolor{keyword}{const} \textcolor{keywordtype}{float} d\_angle = (a\_max -\/ a\_min) / (\textcolor{keywordtype}{float})segments;}
\DoxyCodeLine{10058     \textcolor{keyword}{const} \textcolor{keywordtype}{float} sin\_d = (float)NK\_SIN(d\_angle);}
\DoxyCodeLine{10059     \textcolor{keyword}{const} \textcolor{keywordtype}{float} cos\_d = (float)NK\_COS(d\_angle);}

\DoxyCodeLine{10061     \textcolor{keywordtype}{float} cx = (float)NK\_COS(a\_min) * radius;}
\DoxyCodeLine{10062     \textcolor{keywordtype}{float} cy = (float)NK\_SIN(a\_min) * radius;}
